revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:御廟表塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|name=Gobyo Omotezuka Kofun|image=File:Gobyo-Omotezuka kofun 001.jpg|image_caption=Rear round Kofun| part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}}|location={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1042504|1=Kita-ku, Sakai}}|type={{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|map_size=Mound length: 84.8 meters (estimated restored value based on survey)|burial=Unknown|built=Late 5th century|Special_notes=|coordinates={{Coord|34.56611111111111|N|135.4999388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Gobyo Omotezuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=21.58|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=59.78|omitted=place-of-worship|marksize=14|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=230|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=230|coord={{coord|34|33|21.58|N|135|29|59.78|E}}}}|Cultural_property=National Historic site: part of the Mozu Kofun Group|burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}}} '''Gobyo Omotezuka Kofun''' (御廟表塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Sakai|en|Kita-ku, Sakai|qid=Q1042504|lt=Kita-ku, Sakai|1=Kita-ku, Sakai}} Naka-Mozu Town 4 Chome. It is one of the Kofuns constituting the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. == burial == This mound is a short {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the anterior part oriented westward {{Sfn|Mozu Kofun Group|2014|p=57}}. An aerial photograph taken in 1942 shows that the anterior part extended to the north-south road on the western side of the mound. However, by 1961, the anterior part had been leveled and turned into farmland {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 1|2008|p=33}}. Currently, the anterior part is gone, and the surrounding moat has been partially filled, leaving only a portion around the rear round section {{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/gobyoomotezuka.html|title=Gobyo Omotezuka Kofun|accessdate=2021-dd-20|publisher=Sakai City Office, Cultural Tourism Bureau, Museum Section}}. The rear round section is two-tiered. Excavations have uncovered cylindrical haniwa rows extending from the rear round section toward the anterior part {{Sfn|Mozu Kofun Group|2014|p=57}}. Beneath the mound fill, a layer of grayish silt was laid, a technique also observed in other kofun within the Mozu Kofun Group. This is considered one of the construction methods for mound building. Although unconfirmed, there might have been protruding structures in the constriction area, as lid-shaped haniwa have been found in this vicinity {{Sfn|Mozu Kofun Group|2014|p=57}}. The remaining mound is maintained as a green space Historic site by Sakai City and is open to the public alongside the nearby natural monument of Osaka Prefecture, the {{ill|Kusunoki of Mozu|ja|百舌鳥のくす|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki of Mozu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筒井家住宅|1=Tsutsui House|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q95561513}}, providing a place for relaxation {{Sfn|Mozu Kofun Group|2014|p=57}}. File:Gobyo-Omotezuka kofun 002.jpg|The mound appears like a circular tumulus as the anterior part has been removed (left side was the original anterior part). File:Moat of Gobyo-Omotezuka kofun 001.jpg|The partially remaining moat around the rear round section (Historic site as a national historic site). == area == The estimated restored area of the kofun, based on excavation surveys, are as follows {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 7|2013|p=35}}: * Mound length: 84.8 meters * Rear round section: ** Diameter: 67.6 meters ** Height: 8 meters * Anterior part: ** Width: 32.0 meters == Overview of Surveys == Numerous excavations and radar surveys were conducted to study the mound structure and assess the possibility of burial facilities. Highlights include: * **1987**: The southwestern moat of the rear round section was excavated, identifying a moat 9.6 meters wide and 1.45 meters deep, although no roof tiles or haniwa fragments were found {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 1|2008|p=33}}. * **1994**: Four test excavations were conducted, revealing part of the moat at the anterior section, confirming its preservation in some areas {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 1|2008|p=33}}. * **1999–2000**: Eight excavation sites investigated the leveled anterior part, uncovering cylindrical haniwa rows and remains of the mound foundation {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 1|2008|p=36}}. * **2008**: Ground-penetrating radar surveys identified reflection surfaces indicating the existence of terraces on the rear round section and possible burial facilities {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 3|2010|p=23}}. * **2012**: Eight excavation zones were set to confirm the area of the mound, particularly the second-tier slope base and haniwa rows on the first terrace {{Sfn|Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 7|2013|p=7}}. == burial goods == burial goods associated with this kofun include cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and house-shaped haniwa {{Sfn|built of Kofun|2009|p=128}}. == Historic site == === National Historic site === This kofun is one of 19 Kofuns comprising the Mozu Kofun Group, Historic site as a national historic site. The Historic site area includes the rear round section and a part of the remaining moat on the northeastern corner. * In 2014, this kofun was officially added to the Mozu Kofun Group Historic site alongside other kofun {{Sfn|National Historic site Database|2021}}. * Additional areas within nearby kofun were Historic site in 2018 and 2019, reflecting ongoing efforts to preserve these historic sites. == Access == * {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|qid=Q71986|lt=Nankai Koya Line|1=Koya Line}}: 500 meters on foot from {{ill|Nakamozu Station|en|Nakamozu Station|qid=Q1863087|lt=Nakamozu Station|1=Nakamozu Station}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=-Sakai’s Historic site- Mozu Kofun Group 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Tourism Bureau, Historic site Section|editor=Sakai City Cultural Tourism Bureau}} * {{Cite book|title=Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Education Committee|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section}} * {{Cite book|title=Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 3|date=2010-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Education Committee|editor=Sakai City Mayor’s Office, Cultural Division}} * {{Cite book|title=Investigation of Mozu Kofun Group 7|date=2013-08-31|publisher=Sakai City Education Committee|editor=Sakai City Cultural Tourism Bureau, Historic site Section}} * {{Cite book|title=Special Autumn Exhibition 2009: built of Nintoku-ryo Kofun - Insights from the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|date=2009-09|publisher=Sakai City Museum}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/gobyoomotezuka.html Gobyo Omotezuka Kofun - Sakai City Office] * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/63 Gobyo Omotezuka - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Gobyō Omotezuka Kofun|lt=Gobyō Omotezuka Kofun|lt=Gobyo Omote-zuka Kofun|ja|御廟表塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyo Omote-zuka Kofun|qq=}}
